    "explanation": "The String class in Java is immutable, meaning its value cannot be changed after it is created. Every operation that seems to modify a String actually creates a new String object."

    "correctIndex": 2,
    "explanation": "Using a StringBuilder is the most efficient for multiple concatenations in a single thread because it is mutable and does not create a new object for each append operation, unlike the + operator or concat()."

    "question": "What is the output of `System.out.println("Java" == "Java");` and `System.out.println(new String("Java") == "Java");`?",
    "options": ["true false", "true true", "false false", "false true"],
    "correctIndex": 0,
    "explanation": "The first comparison `\"Java\" == \"Java\"` is true due to string interning; both literals refer to the same object in the string pool.
